title: Paper-guided graph-alignment rebalance

queries: graph soft prompt alignment residual supervision modality mismatch graph llm
graph reconstructive alignment supervision soft prompt graph residual mixing llm

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: The key bottleneck is graph-text modality mismatch, so prompt quality depends on alignment quality rather than prompt token decoration alone.
papers.relevance: Supports shifting Stage 3 effort from prompt-order tinkering toward better basis-query and projector-side alignment.

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: A single compressed graph prompt can lose structural fidelity, so aggressive prompt-side graph injection can become a bottleneck.
papers.relevance: Supports reducing direct graph residual strength instead of increasing prompt-side graph mixing.

papers.title: Toward Graph-Tokenizing Large Language Models with Reconstructive Graph Instruction Tuning
papers.claim: Stronger graph reconstruction-style supervision improves graph-token alignment beyond text-only objectives.
papers.relevance: Supports increasing graph-side basis supervision while keeping the basis channel active at inference.

evidence.why_now: No-basis and basis-loss-only ablations showed the basis path is necessary, while token-only basis showed that removing graph residual entirely helps user-ltv but harms user-churn. The next paper-backed step is therefore not another binary ablation but a rebalance: weaken direct graph residual injection while strengthening graph-side alignment supervision.

hypothesis: The current basis path likely mixes graph-level residuals into prompt space too aggressively relative to how well the graph query is aligned. A better cross-task compromise is to keep token basis residuals unchanged, reduce graph residual strength to about half of the current baseline level, and simultaneously strengthen graph-side alignment supervision with a slightly sharper basis query temperature.
notes: Systematic rule for this first paper-guided scan point: keep basis_residual_alpha unchanged, halve basis_graph_alpha, multiply basis_lambda_g by 1.5, and scale basis_tau to 0.85x the strict-baseline value for each representative task.
This candidate is intentionally limited to graph-alignment rebalance; it does not change prompt ordering, projector LayerNorm, or disable any basis component.
Representative-task gate remains strict: any primary-metric regression kills the bundle.
Launched on 2026-05-05 after fixing stage3_research.py wrapper generation to emit LF line endings and underscore-style CLI flags.
Stopped on 2026-05-05 after orchestrator early-kill recommendation: user-churn regressed on the primary screening metric.
Final report verdict: failed. user-ltv improved, item-incoterms was neutral, but the bundle is globally non-promotable.
